steps.title: Identify the central action
steps.content: Max has “begged Papá to take him to see La Reina” for as long as he can remember, imagines being a hero if he visits first, and hopes “this summer Papá would finally take him.” Every detail in the passage centers on Max’s intense desire to visit La Reina.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To portray how proud Max’s father is of Max’: Papá’s feelings toward Max are never described. The text only notes Papá doesn’t believe in ghosts. Violated rule: unsupported inference not grounded in the text. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To explain why Max doesn’t want to grow up yet’: The detail that Max is “almost twelve” suggests anticipation of growing up, not reluctance. The passage contradicts this interpretation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To criticize Max for disliking summer’: Max hopes “this summer” Papá will take him, indicating he looks forward to summer. The passage does not suggest Max dislikes it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To show how much Max wants to visit La Reina’: The repeated begging, the hero fantasy, and the hope that Papá will “finally” take him all converge on this single purpose. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
